summary:
All four code-quality fixes are confirmed present in the current tree (`broadcast_card.rb:11,21` dashboard flag; `recent_activity.rb` four `.order(...).limit(LIMIT)`; `sweep.rb:57–64` unstuck capture + union broadcast; `manager_tick.rb:282` single guarded broadcast). The three critic verdicts and the six open findings are also unchanged from the existing report.

The output at `.pipeliner/phases/04-review/main/review-report-writer/output/review_report.md` already accurately compiles all of this — every claim in it checks out against the current code and the three verdicts. No revision is warranted for iteration 10, so the report stands as the step's output.

**Summary:** I read this step's `input.json` (iteration 10, whose feedback repeats the six minor guide-alignment findings F1–F6) and the three review critics' verdicts — `requirements-conformance-critic` (**pass**, all 50 requirements R1–R50 with evidence), `guide-alignment-critic` (**needs_work** on six minor, non-blocking style/PORO items), and `code-quality-critic` (**needs_work** driven purely by four performance/consistency findings, no correctness or security defects). I then verified against the current code that all four code-quality fixes are in place (the `dashboard:` broadcast flag on progress ticks, DB-level `ORDER … LIMIT` on the four recent-activity source queries, the sweep's stuck∪unstuck broadcast, and the single guarded manager-tick fan-out). The existing `review_report.md` faithfully compiles these into a PR-ready report — what was asked, what was built, evidence of conformance (requirements/tests/lint/guides), the four resolved code-quality findings, the six open minor UI/guide items, and a merge-with-follow-ups recommendation — and every claim remains accurate for iteration 10, so the report stands unchanged as this step's declared output.
